decisions.decision: Prohibit TaskOutput for foreground team agents
decisions.rationale: cnogo spawns teammates in foreground (default). Foreground agents do not produce TaskOutput entries — they report via TaskList and SendMessage auto-delivery. TaskOutput officially supports agents but is irrelevant for this spawning pattern.
Claude Code uses opaque system-generated task IDs. The model invents name@team_name composites (e.g. plan03-impl@impl-video-purchase) that match no real ID, causing 'No task found' errors.

decisions.decision: Prohibit isolation: worktree on Task tool
decisions.rationale: cnogo manages worktrees via create_session(). Claude Code's isolation: worktree creates separate .cursor/worktrees/ paths that duplicate and conflict with cnogo's worktree session system.
session-cleanup (scripts/memory/worktree.py:cleanup_session) already uses --force for worktree removal. Manual git worktree remove without --force fails on modified/untracked files.

decisions.decision: Explain architectural reason for each prohibition
decisions.rationale: Bare prohibitions leave models guessing. Explaining WHY (foreground spawning, auto-delivery, cnogo worktree ownership) prevents the model from re-inventing the anti-pattern in novel ways.

constraints: team.md word budget ~400 words (enforced by workflow_validate.py)
No changes needed to bridge.py or worktree.py — fix is in command guidance only
